text: A person is exempt from the requirements under ORS 814.485 (Failure to wear protective headgear) and 814.486 (Endangering bicycle operator or passenger) to wear protective headgear, if wearing the headgear would violate a religious belief or practice of the person. [1995 c.581 §4]
markdown:
## Title 59
### Chapter 814. Pedestrians
##### § 814.487 — Exemptions from protective headgear requirements

A person is exempt from the requirements under ORS 814.485 (Failure to wear protective headgear) and 814.486 (Endangering bicycle operator or passenger) to wear protective headgear, if wearing the headgear would violate a religious belief or practice of the person. [1995 c.581 §4]

subsections.text: A person is exempt from the requirements under ORS 814.485 (Failure to wear protective headgear) and 814.486 (Endangering bicycle operator or passenger) to wear protective headgear, if wearing the headgear would violate a religious belief or practice of the person. [1995 c.581 §4]
